title: Non-invertible symmetry enriched string net topological orders
authors: Luisa Eck, Peter Huston, Kyle Kawagoe, David Penneys
abstract: We propose a definition of a non-invertible symmetry enriched topological order (NI-SETO), and we implement our definition for string net models. We do so in two ways, using full inclusions of unitary fusion categories (UFCs), as well as anyon condensation. In both cases, the NI-SETO is a relative center of UFCs. All NI-SETOs can be realized in either model, where we can use enriched UFCs to get chiral examples on the boundary of a 3D Walker-Wang model representing the anomaly. We describe several examples of NI-SETOs and compute the qualitative symmetry action on anyons and symmetry defects using tube algebra techniques.
summary: This paper provides a formal framework for topological orders enriched by non-invertible symmetries. By using string-net models and category theory, it shows how non-invertible symmetries can transform anyons into direct sums of excitations and identifies the resulting twist defects.
main_problem: The paper aims to define and implement Non-Invertible Symmetry Enriched Topological Orders (NI-SETOs), extending the concept of SETOs from group-like symmetries to non-invertible quantum symmetries.
main_result: The authors propose a definition for NI-SETOs using full inclusions of unitary fusion categories and anyon condensation, proving these approaches are dual and can be realized in string-net models.
method: The study utilizes higher category theory, tube algebra techniques, and the graphical calculus of strings to compute symmetry actions and identify twist defects.
model_or_system: The primary models are string-net topological orders, specifically using unitary fusion categories (UFCs), anyon condensation, and 3D Walker-Wang models to represent anomalies.
key_observables: Symmetry action on anyons, types of twist defects, and the multiplicity of anyon transformations under symmetry action.
important_parameters: The specific unitary fusion categories (e.g., Ising, S3, E6) and the choice of condensable algebra A.
main_assumptions: The current definition (Ansatz A) lacks a structure to capture the collection of symmetries independently of a specific UMTC, and the framework assumes semisimplicity.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the relationship between wall excitations, the folding trick, and the connection between categories via twist defects; other diagrams show 3D enriched string-net models and E6 twist defects.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the NI-SETO definition, presents two implementation approaches (full inclusion and anyon condensation), demonstrates their duality, applies these to string-net models, and provides explicit examples like the S3 and E6 cases.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1. E6 twist defects (including anyons) written as free modules on SU(2)10 anyons, and the underlying direct sum of SU(2)10 anyons. The anyon which is not a free module is highlighted in orange.
